Arrowhead puts pressure on Ionis with positive data for triglyceride lowering drug
snippet: With Phase 3 data showing what analysts called a “potentially best-in-class profile,” Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals plans to submit for FDA approval of plozasiran by the end of the year—hoping to follow to market Ionis Pharmaceuticals’ Tryngolza, which got the regulator’s nod in severe hypertriglyceridemia last month.

title: Repligen strikes $1.5B BioLife buyout to bag cell therapy product pharmas rely on
snippet: Top-selling cell therapies from BMS, J&J and Gilead depend on a BioLife product, positioning Repligen to use the takeover to increase its exposure to the modality.

title: 6 companies hiring in Cambridge now
snippet: Looking for a biopharma job in Cambridge? Roles posted on BioSpace have more than doubled compared to last year.

title: US ‘eroding’ lead in rare diseases a matter of national security: Report
snippet: A Congressional advisory panel recommends the U.S. government put supportive policies in place for rare disease drug development, such as a finalized platform designation from the FDA and the allowance of novel trial designs for small patient populations.

title: 2026 IPO stock tracker: How newly public biotechs are performing
snippet: BioSpace is launching an IPO dashboard to help the industry keep tabs on U.S. biotechs that have taken the public plunge in 2026.

title: Novartis scraps mid-stage radiopharma asset as Pluvicto soars
snippet: The discontinued asset, being trialed for breast cancer and solid tumors, had yielded disappointing data, leading to Novartis’ decision to abandon development, according to a company spokesperson.

title: Q2 earnings underway, FDA’s CRL kerfuffle, IPOs full speed ahead in H2
snippet: Second quarter earnings continue to roll with Novartis and Roche reporting this week; Eli Lilly’s AtaiBeckley buy for up to $3.8 billion offers clear “validation” for the fledgling psychedelics space; more drama unfolds at FDA as the agency goes back and forth on its policy of publishing complete response letters in r...

title: From Big Pharma to specialty: why moving closer to the patient changes everything
snippet: Working at a Big Pharma has its benefits, but there are limitations too. SERB Pharmaceuticals’ Thomas Kolaras discusses how moving to a midsize company creates new opportunities for sharpened thinking and leadership.

title: AI data becomes sticking point in biopharma dealmaking
snippet: As drug hunting gets more complex, where and how data is generated in partnerships between Big Pharmas and AI biotechs is becoming a headache for dealmakers.

title: Wainua flop narrows AstraZeneca’s path to $80B goal ahead of high-risk readouts
snippet: With a Phase 3 failure denting the rare disease drug’s prospects, AstraZeneca has little room for error as it plots a path toward its ambitious 2030 sales target.

title: Led By DeepSeek, 10 Frontier Labs Rush Onto The Crunchbase Unicorn Board In June
Samsung Biologics targets $1.8B PolyPeptide buyout to expand in obesity
snippet: With PolyPeptide manufacturing peptides such as GLP-1 medicines, Samsung Biologics has identified the deal as a way to tap into fast-growing demand for obesity drug production services.

title: Agios axes next-gen sickle cell candidate after it fails to best Novo’s asset
snippet: Agios Pharmaceuticals’ sickle cell disease candidate didn’t prove any better than Novo Nordisk’s investigational PK activator or the biopharma’s own asset that is currently under FDA review for the indication.

title: Novartis stays 'geography-agnostic' in dealmaking amid flurry of China deals
snippet: Novartis will continue to be on the lookout for bolt-on deals below $2 billion, CEO Vas Narasimhan said Tuesday on the pharma's second quarter earnings call—though major pickups aren’t completely off the table.

title: The FDA’s 505(b)(2) pathway is an underestimated engine of value creation
snippet: The biopharma industry would do well to re-embrace the 505(b)(2) path, which allows sponsors to draw on previous findings around an existing drug even as they propose modifications to it.

title: Dyne gets early 2027 decision date for Duchenne therapy, analysts expect ‘smooth’ review
snippet: If approved, Dyne Therapeutics’ zeleciment rostudirsen could “capture the majority” of the exon-51 Duchenne muscular dystrophy market, given its better efficacy and dosing profile versus Sarepta Therapeutics’ exon-skipping therapy Exondys 51, the current standard of care, according to Oppenheimer.

title: Axiom plans Hong Kong IPO, hoping to carve new path for US biotechs
snippet: Axiom Biosciences is thinking outside of the box, betting on a public debut in Hong Kong to raise capital and grow into a global company.

title: Lilly’s AtaiBeckley buy validates space as Big Pharma takes note of psychedelics
snippet: Eli Lilly’s proposed acquisition of AtaiBeckley is “the clearest strategic validation to date” of psychedelics as an emerging pharmaceutical category, according to H.C. Wainwright. The analyst cautioned, however, that the transaction should not be seen as “supporting a uniform re-rating of every psychedelic developer.”

title: FDA’s radical transparency policy for rejections runs into legal uncertainty
snippet: Having started to publish CRLs without consulting the public, the FDA is under pressure to change course amid concerns it failed to openly and fully evaluate a complex issue.

title: 2026 IPO class blooms again as BlossomHill, Latigo set sights on Nasdaq
snippet: Eighteen companies have gone public this year so far, putting 2026 on pace to record the highest number of IPOs since the pandemic-driven high of 2021.

title: Vertex’s IgA nephropathy drug derisked after Novartis, Vera approvals
snippet: The FDA is currently reviewing Vertex Pharmaceuticals’ povetacicept, with a target action date of Nov. 30. If approved, the drug could benefit from campaigns from Novartis and Vera Therapeutics for their recently approved IgAN therapies Fabhalta and Trutakna.

title: BIO 2026: Cell and gene therapy sector must break the mold to thrive
snippet: If cell and gene therapy makers are going to achieve their mission to improve patients’ lives, the industry must come together to share information across stakeholders, from regulators to manufacturers to payers.

title: At FDA, ‘caretaker mode’ is better than power trip mode
snippet: UniQure’s planned third-quarter submission for its Huntington’s disease gene therapy may be a harbinger of a more flexible FDA under acting commissioner Kyle Diamantas—but how long will it last? And how can companies be sure these positive decisions won’t just be reversed?

title: Lilly just bought renowned regulator Peter Marks a pipeline to play with
snippet: FDA veteran Peter Marks will now shape the future of Eli Lilly’s vaccines work after the buys of Curevo, LimmaTech Biologics and Vaccine Company for up to $3.8 billion total.

Establishment and characterization of three canine ameloblastoma cell lines with genomic and transcriptomic profiles.
snippet: Establishment of canine acanthomatous ameloblastoma (CAA) cell lines has profound importance in pre-clinical in vitro testing. The goal of this study was to develop, authenticate, and characterize three CAA cell lines. Cell lines were developed with standard cell culture techniques from dogs with naturally occurring CAA. title: Diagnostic and prognostic values of differentially expressed genes in canine mammary carcinoma: An integrated bioinformatics analysis.
snippet: Canine mammary carcinoma (CMC) is a common tumor in unspayed dogs and poses a significant health concern for animals. This study aimed to identify differentially expressed genes (DEGs) between CMC and adjacent healthy mammary tissue through an integrated RNASeq bioinformatics analysis that combined an independently generated dataset from the present study,....

title: Multiomics Analysis Reveals CTHRC1+ CAFs Drive Immunosuppressive Niches and Predict Immunotherapy Resistance in Gastric Cancer.
snippet: Cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) orchestrate immune-excluded tumor microenvironment (TME), but the CAF heterogeneity remains incompletely understood in gastric cancer (GC). title: Cancer-associated fibroblast subtype signature gene predicts survival and immunotherapy response in sarcoma.
snippet: Sarcomas show heterogeneous responses to immune-checkpoint blockade (ICB), and cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) are considered to shape the tumor immune microenvironment, yet CAF programs that predict ICB outcomes in sarcoma remain unclear. title: Associations between self-reported symptoms and circulating protein biomarkers: A scoping review protocol.
snippet: Studies linking self-reported symptoms to circulating protein biomarkers are increasing, partly driven by the rising number of protein biomarkers being identified.
